pairs.question: When was Enter Shikari formed and who were the original members?
pairs.answer: Enter Shikari was formed in 1999 in St Albans, England, by bassist Chris Batten, lead vocalist and keyboardist Rou Reynolds, and drummer Rob Rolfe.

pairs.question: What is Enter Shikari's record label, and how do they distribute their music?
pairs.answer: Enter Shikari operates their own record label called Ambush Reality. To facilitate worldwide distribution, they have signed distribution deals with several major labels.

pairs.question: List Enter Shikari's studio albums in chronological order along with their release dates.
pairs.answer: Enter Shikari's studio albums in chronological order are: Take to the Skies (19 March 2007), Common Dreads (15 June 2009), A Flash Flood of Colour (16 January 2012), The Mindsweep (19 January 2015), The Spark (22 September 2017), Nothing Is True & Everything Is Possible (17 April 2020), A Kiss for the Whole World (21 April 2023), and Lose Your Self (10 April 2026).

pairs.question: What was Enter Shikari's first album to reach number one on the UK charts?
pairs.answer: Enter Shikari's first album to reach number one on the UK charts was A Kiss for the Whole World, released on 21 April 2023.

pairs.question: How did Enter Shikari's debut album perform commercially in the UK?
pairs.answer: Enter Shikari's debut album, Take to the Skies, reached number 4 on the Official UK Album Chart and has since been certified gold in the UK.

pairs.question: What musical styles does Enter Shikari blend in their music?
pairs.answer: Enter Shikari combines influences from rock music genres with those from various electronic music genres, creating an eccentric and distinctive musical style.

pairs.question: Write a Python script to generate a list of Enter Shikari's albums with their release years and chart positions.
pairs.answer:
python
albums = [
    {"title": "Take to the Skies", "release_year": 2007, "chart_position": "Number 4"},
    {"title": "Common Dreads", "release_year": 2009, "chart_position": "Number 16"},
    {"title": "A Flash Flood of Colour", "release_year": 2012, "chart_position": "Number 4"},
    {"title": "The Mindsweep", "release_year": 2015, "chart_position": "N/A"},
    {"title": "The Spark", "release_year": 2017, "chart_position": "N/A"},
    {"title": "Nothing Is True & Everything Is Possible", "release_year": 2020, "chart_position": "N/A"},
    {"title": "A Kiss for the Whole World", "release_year": 2023, "chart_position": "Number 1"},
    {"title": "Lose Your Self", "release_year": 2026, "chart_position": "N/A"}
]

for album in albums:
    print(f"{album['title']} ({album['release_year']}) - Chart Position: {album['chart_position']}")
